verdict_reasoning: TMED9 encodes a p24 family transmembrane cargo receptor that resides in the ER and Golgi membranes, cycling between these compartments to facilitate protein trafficking. Its single-pass TM domain anchors it to endomembrane compartments (ER/Golgi/ERGIC), not the plasma membrane. Pre-no checklist: (1) no clinical cell-surface-directed therapeutic program targets this protein on the plasma membrane; (2) it is not a sheddase substrate with a PM-resident precursor stage; (3) no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to PM documented; (4) no documented minority PM pool — it cycles within the secretory pathway, not to the plasma membrane; (5) no cell-death/cancer ecto-pool, no germline-restricted PM display, no activation-induced PM translocation, no lysosomal exocytosis documented for TMED9 specifically. The dominant biology is endomembrane-resident trafficking protein. No contextual bucket has gene-specific evidence.
